caption: The video appears to be an educational tutorial on cryogenic engineering, specifically focusing on a problem-solving exercise labeled "Tutorial - 1." The screen displays a complex equation for calculating a variable 'y,' which involves enthalpy values (h) and efficiency (ε). A table is presented with various parameters such as pressure (p), temperature (T), enthalpy (h), and entropy (s) for different states labeled 1, 2, f, and g. The values for these parameters are given in units of bar, K, J/g, and J/gK respectively. The calculation shown at the bottom of the screen uses specific values from the table to solve for 'y2,' resulting in a value of 0.060. This tutorial is part of a lecture series by Prof. M D Atrey from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Bombay, as indicated at the bottom of the slide. The video likely explains the step-by-step process of solving this thermodynamic problem related to cryogenic systems.
